ci_support_linux_64_.yaml:
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
fortran_compiler:
- gfortran
fortran_compiler_version:
- '14'
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.description:
I L L U S T R A T E
Biomolecular Illustration copyright 2019 David S Goodsell
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License") you may not use this file except in compliance with the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0. Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
This work was supported in part by Damon Runyon-Walter Winchell Cancer Research Fund Fellowship DRG 972, the US National Institutes of Health R01-GM120604 and the kind support of the RCSB Protein Data Bank. DS Goodsell & AJ Olson (1992) "Molecular Illustration in Black and White" JMolGraphics 10, 235-240. April 2019 Simplified and released with only non-photorealistic rendering

build.script:
set -exo pipefail
export LDFLAGS="${LDFLAGS} -L${BUILD_PREFIX}/lib"
mkdir -p ${PREFIX}/bin
${FC} illustrate.f -o ${PREFIX}/bin/illustrate

raw_meta_yaml:
package:
  name: illustrate
  version: 0.0.0.2024.08.17

source:
  url: https://github.com/ccsb-scripps/Illustrate/archive/e469df4f794f8edbca992a9b2ab534d11d27cdd6.tar.gz
  sha256: c33212a8453985dd1d898ccd0e36e23749c38a1bcac0a64fbce1522ced5f4caf

build:
  number: 1
  skip:
    - win
  script: |
    set -exo pipefail
    export LDFLAGS="${LDFLAGS} -L${BUILD_PREFIX}/lib"
    mkdir -p ${PREFIX}/bin
    ${FC} illustrate.f -o ${PREFIX}/bin/illustrate

requirements:
  build:
    - ${{ compiler("fortran") }}

tests:
  - script:
      - illustrate < 2hhb.inp
    files:
      source:
        - 2hhb.inp
        - 2hhb.pdb

about:
  homepage: https://ccsb.scripps.edu/illustrate
  repository: https://github.com/ccsb-scripps/Illustrate
  documentation: https://ccsb.scripps.edu/illustrate/documentation/
  license: Apache-2.0
  license_file: LICENSE
  summary: "Biomolecular Illustration Tool"
  description: >
    I L L U S T R A T E

    Biomolecular Illustration copyright 2019 David S Goodsell

    Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License") you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
    You may obtain a copy of the License at http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.
    Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    See the License for the specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.

    This work was supported in part by Damon Runyon-Walter Winchell Cancer Research Fund Fellowship DRG 972, the US National Institutes of Health R01-GM120604 and the kind support of the RCSB Protein Data Bank.
    DS Goodsell & AJ Olson (1992) "Molecular Illustration in Black and White" JMolGraphics 10, 235-240. April 2019 Simplified and released with only non-photorealistic rendering

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - eunos-1128
